PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-12-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Triay (Counsel for Robert Mosher, Objector); Nancy Rasch (Counsel for Debra Dolch, Special Administrator and Successor Trustee); David Baer (Counsel for Georgia Albert, Objector); Debra Dolch (Special Administrator and Successor Trustee - present in court).

After brief discussion and consideration as to whether the Trust contest was at issue, the Court referred both matters (the Will Contest, Case No. 289003, and the Trust Contest, Case No. 289070) to Dept. 206 for trial assignment, signing Minute Orders for both cases in open court.

Judicial Office: Dorothy L. McMath, Commissioner; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722; Bailiff: Deputy Rigo Guitron; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: None.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-20-2006 Continued To Feb-26-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: David Baer appearing for petitioner Georgia Lee Albert. Randall Schmidt appearing for petitioner Barbara Bond and for contestant Robert H. Mosher. Will Contest is going forward. Parties have agreed to have Debra Dolch appointed as Special Administrator with full IAEA without bond. Court questions if all parties have waived bond. Court is setting bond in the amount of $10,000.00. Letters will expire until further order of the court. Both petitions are continued to 2/26/07 for status. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Floyd Butler, CSR#2518. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.
